persistent_storage.rationale: Binary columnar format with mmap eliminates text parsing overhead (~10 min → <1 sec for 6M rows). Column pruning saves 50%+ I/O bandwidth (Q1/Q3/Q6 access 6-8 of 16 lineitem columns). Zero-copy mmap access is critical on HDD storage.

columns.name: l_orderkey
columns.notes: FK to orders, used in Q3 join and aggregation. Hash index for join lookups.

columns.name: l_partkey
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

columns.name: l_suppkey
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

columns.name: l_linenumber
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

columns.name: l_quantity
columns.notes: Q6 filter: l_quantity < 24. Q1 aggregation. High entropy, no compression.

columns.name: l_extendedprice
columns.notes: Hot column: all queries compute l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount). High entropy, no compression.

columns.name: l_discount
columns.notes: Q6 filter: BETWEEN 0.05 AND 0.07. All queries use in revenue calculation. Co-locate with l_extendedprice.

columns.name: l_tax
columns.notes: Q1 computes (1 + l_tax). Co-locate with l_extendedprice and l_discount.

columns.name: l_returnflag
columns.notes: Q1 GROUP BY key. Extremely low cardinality (~3 values: 'A', 'N', 'R'). Store as single char, no dictionary needed.

columns.name: l_linestatus
columns.notes: Q1 GROUP BY key. Low cardinality (~2 values: 'F', 'O'). Store as single char.

columns.name: l_shipdate
columns.notes: PRIMARY SORT KEY. Range filters in all queries (Q1: <=, Q3: >, Q6: BETWEEN). Delta encoding reduces 4-byte dates to 1-2 bytes. Zone maps enable 90%+ block skipping.

columns.name: l_commitdate
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload. Delta encode for consistency.

columns.name: l_receiptdate
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload. Delta encode for consistency.

columns.name: l_shipinstruct
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

columns.name: l_shipmode
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

columns.name: l_comment
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

indexes.name: lineitem_shipdate_zonemap
indexes.notes: Per-block min/max for l_shipdate. Sorted data enables 90%+ block skipping on range predicates (Q1, Q3, Q6).

indexes.name: lineitem_orderkey_hash
indexes.notes: Hash index for Q3 join with orders. Pre-size to 1.5M unique orderkeys to avoid rehashing.

columns.name: o_orderkey
columns.notes: PK, used in Q3 join and aggregation. Hash index for customer-orders join.

columns.name: o_custkey
columns.notes: FK to customer. Hash index for join lookup.

columns.name: o_orderstatus
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

columns.name: o_totalprice
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

columns.name: o_orderdate
columns.notes: Q3 filter: o_orderdate < '1995-03-15'. Also GROUP BY key. Sort orders by this column for zone map effectiveness. Delta encoding.

columns.name: o_orderpriority
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

columns.name: o_clerk
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

columns.name: o_shippriority
columns.notes: Q3 GROUP BY key.

columns.name: o_comment
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

indexes.name: orders_orderdate_zonemap
indexes.notes: Per-block min/max for o_orderdate. Sorted data enables effective block skipping for Q3 filter (o_orderdate < '1995-03-15').

indexes.name: orders_orderkey_hash
indexes.notes: Hash index for Q3 join with lineitem. Pre-size to 1.5M entries.

indexes.name: orders_custkey_hash
indexes.notes: Hash index for Q3 join with customer. Pre-size to 150K unique customers.

columns.name: c_custkey
columns.notes: PK, used in Q3 join. Hash index for join.

columns.name: c_phone
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

columns.name: c_acctbal
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

columns.name: c_mktsegment
columns.notes: Q3 filter: c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING'. Low cardinality (~5 values). Dictionary encode to reduce 10-byte strings to 1-byte codes. Evaluate filter on compressed codes.

columns.name: c_comment
columns.notes: Not accessed in current workload.

indexes.name: customer_custkey_hash
indexes.notes: Hash index for Q3 join. Pre-size to 150K entries.

columns.name: s_suppkey
columns.notes: PK. Not accessed in current workload but index for future queries.

columns.name: p_partkey
columns.notes: PK. Not accessed in current workload but index for future queries.

columns.name: ps_partkey
columns.notes: Composite PK (ps_partkey, ps_suppkey). Not accessed in current workload.

columns.name: n_nationkey
columns.notes: PK. Tiny table (25 rows), keep in cache.

columns.name: r_regionkey
columns.notes: PK. Tiny table (5 rows), keep in cache.

index_usage.notes: Filter: l_shipdate <= '1998-12-01' - 90 days. Zone maps skip blocks with min > threshold. Expect 85-90% block skip rate on sorted data.

Q1.parallel_strategy: Morsel-driven parallel scan with 100K-row chunks. Thread-local aggregation into hash tables (3-6 groups), lock-free merge at end. Expected 7-8x speedup on 64 cores (use 8-16 threads to avoid overhead).
Q1.notes: Single-table scan. Zone maps + sorted data enable aggressive block skipping. Delta-encoded l_shipdate reduces date comparison overhead. Fuse scan + filter + projection + aggregation into single operator.

index_usage.notes: Filter: o_orderdate < '1995-03-15'. Zone maps skip blocks with min >= threshold.
Build hash table on filtered customer (c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING'), probe with orders.o_custkey.
Build hash table on filtered orders, probe with lineitem.l_orderkey.

Q3.parallel_strategy: Parallel scan of customer (4 threads) and orders (8 threads). Partitioned parallel hash join: hash-partition both sides by join key, then join partitions independently (8-16 partitions). Final aggregation uses thread-local hash tables with lock-free merge.
Q3.join_ordering: customer (filter c_mktsegment) ⋈ orders (filter o_orderdate) ⋈ lineitem (filter l_shipdate)
Q3.join_ordering_rationale: Filter customer 150K → 30K. Filter orders 1.5M → 600K-900K. Join customer ⋈ orders produces ~30K rows. Join result ⋈ lineitem (6M rows, filtered to ~2.4M) produces ~120K-200K rows. Avoids massive intermediates.
Q3.column_pruning_benefit: customer: 2/8 cols (75% reduction), orders: 4/9 cols (56% reduction), lineitem: 4/16 cols (75% reduction)
Q3.notes: 3-way join. Critical: push down all filters before joins. Dictionary-encoded c_mktsegment enables fast equality check. Use top-K heap (size 10) during aggregation instead of full sort after.

index_usage.notes: Filter: l_shipdate BETWEEN '1994-01-01' AND '1994-12-31'. Zone maps skip blocks outside date range. Sorted data enables near-perfect block pruning (skip ~85-90% of blocks).

Q6.parallel_strategy: Morsel-driven parallel scan with 100K-row chunks. Thread-local accumulation of SUM(l_extendedprice * l_discount), trivial reduction at end. Expected 7-8x speedup. Predicate evaluation order: l_shipdate (zone map), l_discount, l_quantity (cheapest comparisons first).
Q6.notes: Single-table scan with highly selective compound predicate. Zone maps + sorted l_shipdate skip 85-90% of blocks before scanning. SIMD-accelerate remaining filter evaluation (4-8 rows per instruction). Fuse scan + filter + aggregation.

ingestion.parallel_table_strategy: Independent tables (nation, region, supplier, part, customer) ingested concurrently using std::thread pool (5 threads). Then ingest orders and lineitem in parallel (2 threads). Total 7 concurrent ingestion threads initially.
ingestion.parallel_column_strategy: Column writes are sequential within a table (simpler implementation). For large tables (lineitem, orders), use intra-table parallelism instead (see below).
ingestion.intra_table_strategy: For lineitem (6M rows) and orders (1.5M rows): mmap input .tbl file, split into N chunks (N = min(64, num_rows / 100K)), find newline boundaries, parse each chunk in parallel into thread-local column vectors, then concatenate and write sorted binary output. Expected ingestion time: <5 seconds for 6M rows.
ingestion.mmap_input_notes: mmap entire .tbl file for zero-copy input parsing. Use MADV_SEQUENTIAL hint. Avoid ifstream overhead (buffered I/O syscalls).
ingestion.pre_allocation_strategy: Estimate row count from file size: estimated_rows = file_size / avg_bytes_per_line (lineitem: ~150 bytes/line). Pre-allocate column vectors with vec.reserve(estimated_rows) to avoid O(n) reallocations.
ingestion.buffered_write_notes: Use 4MB write buffer per column file (setvbuf). Write entire column vector in one fwrite() call. Reduces syscall overhead on HDD.
ingestion.index_build_strategy: Build zone maps during ingestion by tracking min/max per 100K-row block. For hash indexes, build after sorting but before writing to disk. This avoids separate index-build pass and reduces total ingestion time.
ingestion.sort_strategy: After parsing, sort lineitem by l_shipdate and orders by o_orderdate using parallel sort (std::sort with execution::par_unseq if available, or manual partitioned sort). Sorting 6M rows takes ~2-3 seconds. Sorted output enables zone maps and block skipping.

expected_ingestion_time_seconds.notes: Total ingestion time dominated by lineitem sorting and I/O. Target <15 seconds for SF1 (6M rows). Parallel table ingestion enables overlapping work.

type_mappings.notes: DATE encoded as int32_t days since 1970-01-01 (Unix epoch). DECIMAL stored as double (sufficient precision for TPC-H). CHAR(1) columns (l_returnflag, l_linestatus) stored as single char instead of std::string.

date_encoding_notes: Encode DATE as int32_t = (year - 1970) * 365 + month_offset + day. Delta encoding reduces 4-byte dates to 1-2 bytes in sorted columns. Use precomputed lookup table for date string → int32_t conversion during ingestion.

hardware_config.morsel_size_rationale: 100K rows × 8 columns × 8 bytes/col ≈ 6.4 MB per morsel, fits comfortably in 22 MB L3 cache per socket. Balances parallelism granularity with cache efficiency.
hardware_config.thread_count_scan_rationale: Use 16 threads for large table scans (lineitem, orders). 64 cores available but 16 threads avoid thread creation overhead and scheduler contention. Expected scaling: 14-15x speedup.
hardware_config.thread_count_join_rationale: Partitioned hash join with 8 partitions (one per thread). Avoids excessive partition overhead while enabling parallel build and probe.
hardware_config.hdd_optimization_notes: HDD (rotational=1) has high sequential bandwidth but slow random access. Critical: (1) Sequential I/O via mmap with MADV_SEQUENTIAL, (2) Sort data to cluster related rows, enabling sequential scans even with filtering, (3) Large block sizes (100K rows) reduce seek overhead, (4) Zone maps minimize blocks accessed.

design_rationale.storage_format: Binary columnar with mmap chosen because: (1) Analytical queries access few columns (column pruning saves 50-75% I/O), (2) Zero-copy mmap eliminates text parsing (10 min → <1 sec for 6M rows), (3) HDD storage benefits from sequential access patterns enabled by columnar layout.
design_rationale.sort_keys: Sort lineitem by l_shipdate and orders by o_orderdate because: (1) All queries filter on these date columns with range predicates, (2) Sorted data enables zone maps to skip 85-90% of blocks, (3) Date columns have natural clustering (temporal locality), (4) Trade-off: sorting adds ~3 seconds to ingestion but saves 10-100x on every query execution.
design_rationale.compression: Minimal compression strategy: (1) Delta encoding for sorted date columns (l_shipdate, o_orderdate) reduces 4-byte dates to 1-2 bytes, (2) Dictionary encoding for c_mktsegment (low cardinality, equality filter), (3) No compression for high-entropy numeric columns (l_extendedprice, l_discount) to avoid decompression overhead, (4) Single-char storage for l_returnflag, l_linestatus instead of strings.
design_rationale.indexing: Selective index strategy: (1) Zone maps on date columns (l_shipdate, o_orderdate) for block-level pruning, (2) Hash indexes on join keys (orderkey, custkey) for O(1) lookup, (3) No indexes on unused tables/columns to minimize ingestion time, (4) All indexes built during ingestion to avoid separate rebuild.
design_rationale.block_size: 100K rows per block chosen because: (1) Block data (100K rows × 8 cols × 8 bytes ≈ 6.4 MB) fits in L3 cache (22 MB per socket), (2) Large enough to amortize zone map metadata overhead, (3) Small enough for fine-grained skipping (granularity = 100K rows), (4) Matches morsel size for parallel execution.
design_rationale.parallel_ingestion: Multi-level parallelism: (1) Parallel table ingestion (7 concurrent tables initially) leverages 64 cores, (2) Intra-table parallelism (split lineitem into chunks) for largest tables, (3) mmap input + pre-allocation + buffered writes optimize single-table ingestion, (4) Target <15 seconds for SF1 ingestion, amortized across many query executions.
design_rationale.io_strategies: Per-query optimization: (1) Q1 and Q6: single-table scans with aggressive column pruning (56-75% reduction) and zone map skipping (85-90% blocks), (2) Q3: careful join ordering (filter customer → 30K, orders → 600K, then join) with filter pushdown and hash indexes, (3) All queries use thread parallelism (7-15x speedup) and operator fusion to minimize materialization.
design_rationale.optimization_target: Execution time optimization prioritizes: (1) Minimizing I/O via column pruning and block skipping (memory bandwidth is bottleneck), (2) Maximizing parallelism with morsel-driven execution (utilize 64 cores), (3) Avoiding bad join orders and unnecessary work (filter pushdown, top-K heaps), (4) Cache-friendly data layouts (columnar, sorted, 100K-row blocks fit in L3).

summary: Storage design optimized for TPC-H workload on 64-core HDD system. Binary columnar format with mmap for zero-copy access. Sort lineitem by l_shipdate and orders by o_orderdate; zone maps enable 85-90% block skipping on range queries. Delta encoding for dates, dictionary for low-cardinality strings. Hash indexes on join keys. 100K-row blocks fit in L3 cache. Column pruning saves 50-75% I/O. Parallel ingestion (<15 sec for 6M rows) with mmap input and pre-allocation. Per-query strategies: Q1/Q6 use zone maps + parallel scan (7-8x speedup), Q3 uses filter pushdown + hash join ordering (10-100x vs naive). Expected overall speedup: 10-15x over text parsing + single-threaded row-store execution.
